children.heading: Advanced solid-state lighting
children.label: 1 Advanced solid-state lighting
children.text: The term “advanced solid-state lighting” means a semiconducting device package and delivery system that produces white light using externally applied voltage.

children.heading: Industry Alliance
children.label: 2 Industry Alliance
children.text: The term “Industry Alliance” means an entity selected by the Secretary under subsection (d).

children.heading: Initiative
children.label: 3 Initiative
children.text: The term “Initiative” means the Next Generation Lighting Initiative carried out under this section.

children.heading: Research
children.label: 4 Research
children.text: The term “research” includes research on the technologies, materials, and manufacturing processes required for white light emitting diodes.

children.heading: White light emitting diode
children.label: 5 White light emitting diode
children.text: The term “white light emitting diode” means a semiconducting package, using either organic or inorganic materials, that produces white light using externally applied voltage.

children.heading: Initiative
children.label: b Initiative
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a Next Generation Lighting Initiative in accordance with this section to support research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities related to advanced solid-state lighting technologies based on white light emitting diodes.

children.heading: Objectives
children.label: c Objectives
children.text: The objectives of the Initiative shall be to develop advanced solid-state organic and inorganic lighting technologies based on white light emitting diodes that, compared to incandescent and fluorescent lighting technologies, are longer lasting, are more energy-efficient and cost-competitive, and have less environmental impact.

children.heading: Industry Alliance
children.label: d Industry Alliance
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary shall competitively select an Industry Alliance to represent participants who are private, for-profit firms, open to large and small businesses, that, as a group, are broadly representative of United States solid-state lighting research, development, infrastructure, and manufacturing expertise as a whole.August 8, 2005

children.label: A
children.text: researchers, including Industry Alliance participants;

children.label: B
children.text: small businesses;

children.label: C
children.text: National Laboratories; and

children.label: D
children.text: institutions of higher education.

children.label: A
children.text: comments to identify solid-state lighting technology needs;

children.label: B
children.text: an assessment of the progress of the research activities of the Initiative; and

children.label: C
children.text: assistance in annually updating solid-state lighting technology roadmaps.

children.heading: Availability to public
children.label: 3 Availability to public
children.text: The information and roadmaps under paragraph (2) shall be available to the public.

children.heading: Development, demonstration, and commercial application
children.label: f Development, demonstration, and commercial application

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a development, demonstration, and commercial application program for the Initiative through competitively selected awards.

children.heading: Preference
children.label: 2 Preference
children.text: In making the awards, the Secretary may give preference to participants in the Industry Alliance.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: g Cost sharing
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall require cost sharing in accordance with .section 16352 of this title

that the Industry Alliance participants who are active participants in research, development, and demonstration activities related to the advanced solid-state lighting technologies that are covered by this section shall be granted the first option to negotiate with the invention owner, at least in the field of solid-state lighting, nonexclusive licenses and royalties on terms that are reasonable under the circumstances;

children.label: A
children.text: that, for 1 year after a United States patent is issued for the invention, the patent holder shall not negotiate any license or royalty with any entity that is not a participant in the Industry Alliance described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: that, during the year described in subparagraph (A), the patent holder shall negotiate nonexclusive licenses and royalties in good faith with any interested participant in the Industry Alliance described in paragraph (1); and
such other terms as the Secretary determines are required to promote accelerated commercialization of inventions made under the Initiative.

children.heading: National Academy review
children.label: i National Academy review
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into an arrangement with the National Academy of Sciences to conduct periodic reviews of the Initiative.
